widgetData.source_sentence: What were the results of the functional study using RNA and cDNA derivatives of the BRCA1 c.5074+3A>C variant?
sentences: The patient was discharged on [date of discharge] and his most recent HbA1c level was 7.1% with a few episodes of hypoglycemia.

The patient was diagnosed with stage IV NSCLC (malignant pleural effusion) in December 2014. Adenocarcinoma cells from pleural effusion were found, and immunohistochemistry analysis demonstrated positivity in TTF-1 and negativity in CK 5/6 and P63.
Based on the results of the functional study using RNA and cDNA derivatives of the BRCA1 c.5074+3A>C variant, it is a likely pathogenic variant.
The patient underwent urgent off-pump coronary artery bypass surgery with LIMA and SVGs due to his medical comorbidities.
Treatment options discussed included calcium channel antagonists, nitrates, pneumatic dilation, botulinum toxin injection, and parenteral nutrition (TPN).
Intravitreal bevacizumab (1.25 mg) injection was used as the primary treatment, followed by a second injection with conventional laser photocoagulation.

widgetData.source_sentence: What were the results of the autopsy?
sentences: Serial doubling dilution titration was done to determine titers of anti-D, anti-C, and anti-E.

Postmortem CT examination revealed widespread consolidations in both lung fields with sporadic alveolar hemorrhages, while autopsy specimens taken from the lungs showed findings consistent with diffuse alveolar damage.
An autopsy was performed with the informed consent of the family. No further details were available.
The patient was advised to keep a follow-up visit with the physician after discharge. The patient should contact the hospital immediately if the symptoms reoccur or worsen.

The patient will follow up with his primary care physician for further management of his hypertension and type II diabetes mellitus. He will also be referred for further outpatient rehabilitation to continue improving his function.

The patient was advised to follow up regularly with his oncologist to monitor his condition and response to therapy. He was also instructed to follow up with his primary care physician as needed for management of his hypertension, ischemic heart disease, and benign prostatic hyperplasia.

widgetData.source_sentence: What was the outcome of the second surgical debulking procedure?
sentences: The patient is advised to seek medical attention if they experience any COVID-19 related symptoms, such as fever, cough, and dyspnea.
The patient last followed-up two months after surgery. Proptosis had completely subsided but the patient did have mild ptosis. Nevertheless, the patient was very satisfied with the outcome.
Although the treatment initially showed signs of efficacy, the tumor progressed rapidly, and the patient died three months after the second surgical debulking procedure.
